were [film production](/wiki/Film_production "Film production") [studios](/wiki/Film_studio "Film studio") located in the Italian capital [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"). They were established on Via Veio in 1930 by [Stefano Pittaluga](/wiki/Stefano_Pittaluga "Stefano Pittaluga"), head of the [Cines](/wiki/Cines "Cines") film company, at the beginning of the [sound era](/wiki/Sound_era "Sound era"). It produced Italy's first sound film *[The Song of Love](/wiki/The_Song_of_Love_(1930_film) "The Song of Love (1930 film)")* the same year. For several years it was the leading studio complex in Italy, until September 1935, when it suffered a major fire and was largely destroyed. This became a spur for the Italian government of [Benito Mussolini](/wiki/Benito_Mussolini "Benito Mussolini") to invest in the construction of a new development [Cinecittà](/wiki/Cinecitt%C3%A0 "Cinecittà"), the largest studio in Europe which opened in 1937.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian film company

The **Cines Studios** were [film production](/wiki/Film_production "Film production") [studios](/wiki/Film_studio "Film studio") located in the Italian capital [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"). They were established on Via Veio in 1930 by [Stefano Pittaluga](/wiki/Stefano_Pittaluga "Stefano Pittaluga"), head of the [Cines](/wiki/Cines "Cines") film company, at the beginning of the [sound era](/wiki/Sound_era "Sound era"). It produced Italy's first sound film *[The Song of Love](/wiki/The_Song_of_Love_(1930_film) "The Song of Love (1930 film)")* the same year. For several years it was the leading studio complex in Italy, until September 1935, when it suffered a major fire and was largely destroyed. This became a spur for the Italian government of [Benito Mussolini](/wiki/Benito_Mussolini "Benito Mussolini") to invest in the construction of a new development [Cinecittà](/wiki/Cinecitt%C3%A0 "Cinecittà"), the largest studio in Europe which opened in 1937.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The refurbished Cines studios continued to operate until 1956, but were often rented out for the use of other companies.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
